frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: decisions arise from integrating sensory evidence to a commitment threshold

frameworks.name: commitment / bounded decision
frameworks.one_line: decisions terminate via a commitment process that can be flexibly modulated

frameworks.name: temporal dependency / sequential decision-making
frameworks.one_line: static accumulation models extended to handle changing expectations and goals

core_question: How does the brain implement flexible visual decision-making when expectations, contingencies, and goals evolve over time?
key_assumptions: decisions integrate immediate sensory input with past experience and future goals
evidence accumulation and commitment mechanisms underlie visual choice
decision processes can operate flexibly across multiple timescales
key_findings: visual decision-making relies on evidence accumulation and commitment mechanisms
these mechanisms, well-characterized under static conditions, can operate more flexibly
decisions are embedded within streams of temporal dependencies rather than isolated
interpretations: a deeper account requires identifying principles governing flexibility across timescales
effective decisions in dynamic environments depend on temporally-embedded mechanisms
